---
format:
  revealjs:
    progress: false
    theme: [default, styles.scss]
    include-after-body: progressor.html
    slideNumber: true
execute: 
  echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
revealjs-plugins:
  - editable
filters:
  - editable
---

## PPG II and beyond: Building an open, community driven taxonomic system for ferns and lycophytes {data-background-image="images/Picture1.png" data-background-size="contain" background-position="right" style="width: 50%; margin-left: -65px;"}

::: {style="width: 100%; font-size: 0.7em;"}
**Joel H. Nitta** (PPG Organizing Committee)

Graduate School of Global and Transdisciplinary Studies

Chiba University, Japan

:::

```{r}
source("R/packages.R")
source("R/functions.R")
```


## Outline

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Introduction to PPG
- PPG II Phase 1
- PPG II Phase 2
- Conclusion
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/qr_code.png){height="400"}

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em"}
Access slides here ⬆︎
:::

:::

::::

# Introduction to PPG {data-part="Intro to PPG"}

## Taxonomies are the *lingua franca* of biologists

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

- Taxonomies give us a **"common language"** to share ideas about species

:::
::: {.column}
![](images/kyotsu-gengo_1.png){height="450"}
:::
:::

## Taxonomies are the *lingua franca* of biologists

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

- Taxonomies give us a **"common language"** to share ideas about species

- Without it, we don’t know what we’re talking about!

:::
::: {.column}
![](images/kyotsu-gengo_2.png){height="450"}
:::
:::

## Taxonomies are the *lingua franca* of biologists

:::::: {.incremental}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- The APG (Angiosperm Phylogeny Group) taxonomy is critical for botanists, especially those working on **flowering plants**

- What about **pteridophytes (ferns and lycophytes)**?

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/apg.png){height="450"}
:::

::::

::::::

## Taxonomies are the *lingua franca* of biologists

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- The APG (Angiosperm Phylogeny Group) taxonomy is critical for botanists, especially those working on **flowering plants**

- What about **pteridophytes (ferns and lycophytes)**?
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/apg-no-ferns.png){height="450"}
:::

::::

## Pteridophytes have the PPG (Pteridophyte Phylogeny Group)

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
- Had a late start
  - APG: 1998, 4 major updates
  - PPG: 2016, **no updates yet**

- Community-based approach
  - APG: 13 authors
  - PPG: 94 authors
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/ppg-2016.png){height="400"}
:::

::::

## PPG is based on **phylogeny** {data-background-image="images/tree-back.png" data-background-size="auto 85%" data-background-position="right" style="width: 80%;"}

- Primary criterion for recognizing taxa is **monophyly**

## PPG is based on **phylogeny** {data-background-image="images/tree-papers-back.png" data-background-size="auto 85%" data-background-position="right" style="width: 80%;"}

- Primary criterion for recognizing taxa is **monophyly**

- Based on many studies conducted at lower taxonomic levels

## Towards PPG II

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Since 2016, ca. **80 new names** (genus and above) have been published

- We need to **discuss and decide** whether to accept these names or not

- Work towards PPG II, including names at genus and above started in May 2023 (**Phase I**)
:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::


::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/sitobolium.png){height="400"}


::: {style="font-size: 0.5em"}
***Sitobolium*** Gen. nov.（previously *Dennstaedtia*）  
Triana-Morena et al. (2022)
:::

:::

::::

## PPG II community

```{r}
ppg2_authors <- read_csv("data/ppg2_author_list.csv")

n_auth <- ppg2_authors |>
  pull(name) |>
  n_distinct()

n_country <- ppg2_authors |>
  pull(country) |>
  n_distinct()

```

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="25%"}
- **`r n_auth`** active members representing **`r n_country`** countries

- Coordinating committee of 5 members

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em"}
At International Botanical Congress XX in Madrid, Spain (2024)
:::

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4
make_author_map(ppg2_authors)
```

![](images/spain-meeting.png){height="250"}

:::

::::

# PPG II Phase 1 {data-part="PPG II Phase 1"}

## A new discussion platform for PPG II


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
- PPG I used **email** as as primary discussion tool
  - Not open
  - No permanent record
:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
![](images/email-bad.png){height="150"}
:::

::::

## A new discussion platform for PPG II

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
- PPG I used **email** as as primary discussion tool
  - Not open
  - No permanent record

- PPG II uses **GitHub**
  - All discussions are **open** and available in perpetuity
:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
![](images/email-bad.png){height="150"}
![](images/github-good.png){height="150"}
:::

::::

## List of proposals (“issues”) is available at <https://github.com/pteridogroup/ppg>

![](images/issues.png){}

## **Anyone** can submit a proposal
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Conditions
  - Must be a change relative to PPG I
  - Must be **validly published** according to the International Code of Nomenclature
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/issue-detail-1.png)
:::

::::

## All discussions are **open**

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Anybody can view and comment
- There is a **1-month discussion** period before voting
:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/issue-detail-2.png)
:::

::::

## Voting by Google forms
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
- A $2/3$ majority is required to pass

![](images/sahashia.jpg){height="250"}

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em"}
***Sahashia*** Gen. nov.（formerly *Botrychium*）  
Photo: V.S. Volkotrub <https://www.gbif.org/occurrence/2005351333>
:::

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/voting.png){height="350"}
:::

::::

## We have reached the goal for proposals

```{r}
ppg_issues <- read_csv("data/ppg_issues.csv")
make_issues_plot(ppg_issues)
```

Since May 2023, **79** proposals were submitted and voted on (goal: 80 taxa)

## PPG II is **complete**

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Submitted to *TAXON* on March 4, 2026

- Includes **`r n_auth`** authors representing **`r n_country`** countries
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/ppg2-ms-authors-short.png){}  
...  
![](images/ppg2-ms-classification-short.png){}
:::

::::

---

## Overview of the PPG II taxonomy 

<br>

::: {style="font-size: 0.9em;"}

```{r}
read_csv("data/ppg_taxa_count.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  select(taxonRank, accepted, synonym, ppgi_accepted) |>
  filter_out(taxonRank %in% c("Tribe", "Nothogenus", "Species")) |>
  select(-synonym) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      c(accepted, ppgi_accepted),
      ~ str_remove_all(.x, "\\*|\\\\")
    )
  ) |>
  select(
    Rank = taxonRank,
    `PPG II` = accepted,
    `PPG I` = ppgi_accepted
  ) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

:::

# PPG II Phase 2 {data-part="PPG II Phase 2"}

## Phase 2: Curation of species-level data

- PPG II includes data to **species** (PPG I only treated to genus)

- There are too many species to vote on every change

- Instead, PPG members volunteer as **Curators** who are responsible for species within their taxon of expertise (usually genus or family)

## PPG manages data in parternship with WFO

- [**World Flora Online（WFO）**](https://www.worldfloraonline.org/) is a **global database for plant names**

- PPG manages data for pteridophytes in WFO

- WFO provides a taxonomic database editor called **"Rhakhis"**

![](images/wfo.png){height="150"}

## Collaborative taxonomic editing in Rhakhis

- Rhakhis enforces **taxonomic rules**

- All editing done online, **in the cloud**

![](images/rhakhis.png){height="350"}

## Benefits of the open, community-based approach
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Lessens the workload
- Ensures **high-quality data**
- **More frequent updates**
  - We don't have to wait another 10 years!!
  - PPG 2.0.1, 2.0.2, etc.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/computer_mob_programming.png){}
:::

::::

```{r}
# Load curator data
curators <- read_csv("data/curators.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

curators_long <- curators |>
  select(genus, contains("curator")) |>
  pivot_longer(names_to = "num", values_to = "name", -genus)

n_curators <- curators_long |>
  filter_out(is.na(name)) |>
  pull(name) |>
  n_distinct()

gen_count <-
  curators_long |>
  group_by(genus) |>
  summarize(
    n = n_distinct(name, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    has_curator = case_when(
      n > 0 ~ "yes",
      .default = "no"
    )
  ) |>
  count(has_curator) %>%
  split(.$has_curator)

genera_coverage <- round(
  gen_count$yes$n / sum(gen_count$yes$n, gen_count$no$n) * 100,
  1
)
```

## The majority of genera have at least one curator

```{r}
make_curators_plot(curators)
```

Since starting Feb. 17 2026, we already have **`r n_curators`** curators covering **`r genera_coverage`%** genera

# Conclusion {data-part="Conclusion"}

## Places to find PPG

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- [World Flora Online](https://www.worldfloraonline.org/)
  - Snapshots updated twice a year
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/wfo_shade.png){}
:::

::::

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- [PPG Website](https://pteridogroup.github.io/ppg.html)
  - More frequent updates
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/ppg-class-shade.png){}
:::

::::

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- [Rhakhis](https://list.worldfloraonline.org/rhakhis/ui/index.html)
  - Live data
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/rhakhis-shade.png){}
:::

::::

## Summary

- PPG takes an **open**, **community-based** approach to taxonomic classification

- Thanks to this, we are able to provide **high-quality**, **up-to-date** data

::: {style="border: 2px solid #333; padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px; background-color: #f9f9f9; font-size: 0.8em;"}

### Acknowledgements

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Alan Elliott (WFO)
- Roger Hyam (WFO)
- Organizers of IBC XX
- Oranizers of Botany 2025
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/qr_code.png){height="210" style="margin-top: -30px; margin-left: 60px;"}
:::

::::

:::
